---
title: "4.5 Rotate and Reverse"
subtitle: "Implementing Array Algorithms"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Rotating Array Elements

### Shift right or left

Rotating an array means shifting all the elements in the array to the right or left by one position.

For example, if the array is `{6, 2, 5, 3}`, rotating it to the right gives `{3, 6, 2, 5}`.

To rotate an array, copy the last element to the first position and then copy all the other elements to the next position.

Use a temporary variable to store the last element before overwriting it.

## Mixed-Up Code {.fit-small}

### `parsonsprob:: rotateRightParsons`

The method should return an integer array that is rotated to the right, so `{6, 2, 5, 3}` returns `{3, 6, 2, 5}`.

The method return type is `int[]`, which means it will return an `int` array.

```java
// Block A
public static int[] rotateRight(int[] arr)
{

// Block B
int[] result = new int[arr.length];

// Block C
result[0] = arr[arr.length - 1];

// Block D
for (int i = 0; i < arr.length - 1; i++)
{
```

## Mixed-Up Code {.fit-small}

### rotateRightParsons continued

```java

// Distractor
for (int i = 0; i < arr.length; i++)
{

// Block E
result[i + 1] = arr[i];

// Block F
} //end for loop

// Block G
return result;

// Block H
} //end shiftRight method
```

## Correct Order

### Rotate right into a new array

```java
public static int[] rotateRight(int[] arr)
{
    int[] result = new int[arr.length];
    result[0] = arr[arr.length - 1];
    for (int i = 0; i < arr.length - 1; i++)
    {
        result[i + 1] = arr[i];
    } //end for loop
    return result;
} //end shiftRight method
```

## Rotate In Place

### Array reference changes the original array

We can also rotate in place without constructing another array.

We need to use an indexed loop to access the elements at different indices.

When you pass an array to a method, you are actually passing a reference to the array's memory location.

This means that any changes made to the array within the method will affect the original array.

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: rotate`

The code below rotates array elements to the left in place without constructing another array.

Can you change it to rotate the elements to the right instead?

Hint: use a backwards loop.

## rotate Source Code

::: {.code-scroll}
```java
public class Rotate
{
    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        int[] values = {6, 2, 1, 7, 12, 5};
        int first = values[0];
        for (int i = 0; i < values.length; i++)
        {
            // if it's not the last element, copy the next one over
            if (i < values.length - 1)
            {
                values[i] = values[i + 1];
            }
            else
            {
                // last element gets first
                values[i] = first;
            }
        }
        // print them out to see the results
        for (int val : values)
        {
            System.out.print(val + " ");
        }
    }
}
```
:::

## rotate Test Targets

### Runestone checks

Expected output after changing the code to rotate right:

```text
5 6 2 1 7 12
```

The test also checks for this assignment:

```java
values[i] = values[i - 1]
```

## Reversing an Array

### Swap from both ends

Reversing an array is similar to rotating it.

We can copy the first element to the last position, the second element to the second-to-last position, and so on.

Use a temporary variable to store the value of the element being overwritten.

Arrays are passed as references to methods, so changes made within the method affect the original array.

## Swap Pattern {.image-fit}

### Temporary variable

![Swapping elements in an array](../../assets/4-data-collections/4-5-implementing-array-algorithms/swap.png){fig-align="center" width="34%"}

```java
// Swapping 2 elements at index i and j
int temp = array[i];  // pour milk into temp cup
array[i] = array[j];  // pour orange juice into milk glass
array[j] = temp;      // pour milk from temp into oj glass
```

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: parsons_array_reverse`

The method reverses an array in place using an indexed `for` loop and a temp variable.

```java
// Block A
public static void reverse(int[] a)
{

// Block B
int temp = 0;
int half = a.length / 2;
int max = a.length - 1;

// Block C
for (int i = 0; i < half; i++)
{
```

## Mixed-Up Code

### parsons_array_reverse continued

```java

// Block D
temp = a[i];

// Block E
a[i] = a[max - i];

// Block F
a[max - i] = temp;

// Block G
} // end for

// Block H
} // end method
```

## Correct Order

### Reverse with indexed for loop

```java
public static void reverse(int[] a)
{
    int temp = 0;
    int half = a.length / 2;
    int max = a.length - 1;
    for (int i = 0; i < half; i++)
    {
        temp = a[i];
        a[i] = a[max - i];
        a[max - i] = temp;
    } // end for
} // end method
```

## Mixed-Up Code

### `parsonsprob:: parsons_array_reverse2`

The method reverses an array in place using a `while` loop and a temp variable.

It includes two extra blocks that are not needed in a correct solution.

```java
// Block A
public static void reverse(int[] array)
{

// Block B
int start = 0;
int end = array.length - 1;

// Block C
while (start < end)
{
```

## Mixed-Up Code

### parsons_array_reverse2 continued

```java

// Distractor
for(int i = start; i < end; i++)
{

// Block D
int temp = array[start];

// Block E
array[start] = array[end];

// Block F
array[end] = temp;

// Block G
start++;
end--;

// Distractor
start++;

// Block H
}
}
```

## Correct Order

### Reverse with while loop

```java
public static void reverse(int[] array)
{
    int start = 0;
    int end = array.length - 1;
    while (start < end)
    {
        int temp = array[start];
        array[start] = array[end];
        array[end] = temp;
        start++;
        end--;
    }
}
```

## Coding Exercise

### `activecode:: reverseArrayCode`

Create a method `reverse` that reverses an array in place using a temp variable.

## reverseArrayCode Starter

```java
import java.util.Arrays;

public class ReverseTest
{
    public static void reverse(String[] array)
    {
        // TODO: reverse array in place using a temp variable to swap elements

    }

    public static void main(String[] args)
    {
        String[] a1 = {"p","a","w","s"};
        reverse(a1);
        System.out.println(Arrays.toString(a1));
    }
}
```

## reverseArrayCode Test Targets

### Runestone checks

Expected output from `main`:

```text
[s, w, a, p]
```

Additional method test:

```text
reverse({"r", "a", "t", "s"}) -> [s, t, a, r]
```

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- rotate right by saving the last element before overwriting
- use a backwards loop when rotating right in place
- explain that array parameters are references to the original array
- swap array values using a temporary variable
- stop reverse loops at the midpoint or when `start < end` becomes false
- update both indexes when reversing with a `while` loop
:::

## End

### 4.5 Part 4 complete

Next: using text files.
